plan_summary.plan_name: EU Cross-Border Rail Single Through-Ticketing Program
plan_summary.plan_type: Public-sector regulatory and infrastructure coordination program
plan_summary.primary_goal: Achieve 40% of eligible cross-border journeys sold as single through-tickets within five years via OSDM standardization and inter-carrier clearing.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Five-year EU coordination program with a EUR 1.5B public budget; the model evaluates budget allocation coverage, clearing-float adequacy versus peak daily obligation, expected through-ticket volume and clearing revenue at the 40% adoption gate, and governance personnel burn.

key_values.label: Public coordination budget
key_values.comment: Five-year EU public envelope funding standards, testing, grants, clearing, and reference distributor.
key_values.source_text: Public coordination budget is EUR 1.5 billion - public funding input.

key_values.label: Capacity building grants
key_values.comment: 60% of the public budget earmarked for national operator IT grants and testing support.

key_values.label: Public reference distributor budget
key_values.comment: 10% of public budget ring-fenced for the public reference distributor's establishment and five-year running costs.

key_values.label: Emergency Float Reserve
key_values.comment: Ring-fenced reserve triggered when clearing float falls below 150% of peak daily obligation.
key_values.source_text: Ring-fenced EUR 300 million Emergency Float Reserve available if operational float drops below 150% of peak daily obligation.

key_values.label: Initial clearing mechanism float
key_values.comment: Minimum required operational float for the inter-carrier clearing mechanism at T+3 settlement.
key_values.source_text: Initial float set at EUR 200 million based on busiest corridors.

key_values.label: Through-ticket adoption target
key_values.comment: Share of eligible cross-border journeys that must be sold as single through-tickets within five years.
key_values.source_text: Success target is 40% of cross-border journeys sold as single tickets.

key_values.label: Governance and testing FTEs
key_values.comment: Dedicated headcount for first two years across standards, conformance testing, and stakeholder relations.
key_values.source_text: Total required FTEs: 35 (Standards: 10, Testing Coordination: 15, Relations: 10).

key_values.label: Liquidity safety multiple
key_values.comment: Operational float must stay at or above 150% of peak daily clearing obligation; below this triggers reserve.
key_values.source_text: Collateral drops below 150% of peak daily obligation.

derived_questions.question: Is expected annual clearing-fee revenue sufficient to sustain the float without public top-up?
derived_questions.why_it_matters: Settlement viability post initial float depends on transactional fee velocity outpacing the float-depletion curve.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Annual eligible cross-border journeys
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Denominator that converts the 40% adoption target into an absolute through-ticket volume.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Use Eurostat cross-border passenger rail statistics for priority corridors as a baseline; cross-check with operator reported volumes.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Peak daily clearing obligation
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Drives the 150% liquidity trigger and the Emergency Float Reserve buffer calculation.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Estimate from peak-day cross-border ticket sales times average ticket value across all distributors.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Average governance FTE monthly cost
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Required to budget two-year personnel cost for the 35 governance FTEs separately from IT grants.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Benchmark against EU agency loaded-cost averages for senior technical and policy staff in Brussels.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Clearing transactional fee per ticket
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Capitalization of the clearing mechanism relies on the per-ticket fee; needed for clearing revenue projection.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Benchmark against PSD2 interchange fees and existing rail distribution per-segment fees.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Expected annual through-ticket volume at target
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Converts the headline 40% adoption gate into a volume that can drive revenue and clearing-velocity tests.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Expected annual clearing fee revenue
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Sizes fee-funded capitalization of the clearing mechanism against the float requirement.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Emergency float buffer over 150% trigger
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Tests whether the EUR 300M reserve actually covers the 150%-of-peak liquidity trigger.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Unallocated public budget surplus
recommended_first_calculations.formula_hint: budget_allocation_surplus_eur = total_public_coordination_budget_eur - capacity_building_grants_eur - reference_distributor_budget_eur - emergency_float_reserve_eur - initial_clearing_float_eur
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Shows residual headroom in the EUR 1.5B envelope after named allocations; positive means slack for governance, contingencies, or top-ups.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Two-year governance personnel cost
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Sizes the dedicated operational burn separate from the IT grants envelope; checks against budget surplus.

unmodelled_gates.label: DG MOVE legal mandate for OSDM exposure
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Mandatory API exposure and three-strikes enforcement require legal authority from DG MOVE; without it, no compliance lever exists.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Operators face no enforceable obligation, three-strikes is unusable, and the 40% adoption target collapses.

unmodelled_gates.label: Political acceptance of three-strikes enforcement
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Suspending sales for SNCF, DB, or OEBB risks coordinated political resistance the model cannot quantify.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Enforcement is blocked at Council level, allowing non-compliance to persist past the 18- and 30-month deadlines.

unmodelled_gates.label: Legal defensibility of tiered liability model
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Strict tiered liability must survive operator legal challenge under EU passenger rights law; this is a binary legal outcome.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Backstop architecture collapses, forcing renegotiation and stalling rollout on contested corridors.

unmodelled_gates.label: Authorization to operate centralized clearing/settlement float
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Managing the centralized clearing float requires specific regulatory authorization that the model treats as given.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Clearing mechanism cannot legally hold the EUR 200M float, blocking single-payment settlement entirely.
